rulings.Case Title: LETICIA C CORAL VS. TRICIA ARVEDI
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 10, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT TRICIA ARVEDI, MULLINS AND COMPANY MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted with ten days leave, if plaintiff can do so in good faith, to file a fourth amended complaint alleging facts sufficient to support an award of punitive damages. The primary allegations in the exemplary damages attachment are alleged in terms of "Defendants knew or should have known¿." This is insufficient to support a showing of conscious disregard. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ELIDA BARILLAS et al VS. AVAIRPROS SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S Demand For Production Of Documents (Set Ons. Two And Four) And Special Interrogatories (Set No. Two); Req. For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, January 10, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ELIDA BARILLAS, MARIO GOMEZ Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Demand For Production Of Documents (Set One Two And Four) And Special Interrogatories (Set No. Two); Req. For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. Pro Tem Judge Renne has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party must bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ELIDA BARILLAS et al VS. AVAIRPROS SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend The Complaint To Add A Plea For Punitive Damages Against Thyssenkrupp Airport Systems
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 10, 2012, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ELIDA BARILLAS, MARIO GOMEZ Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend The Complaint To Add A Plea For Punitive Damages Against Thyssenkrupp Airport Systems is Granted. Plaintiffs have shown good cause for the amendment and defendant ThyssenKrupp has not shown any prejudice that could not be ameliorated by a short trial continuance. If defendant desires a short continuance, its counsel should meet and confer with plaintiffs' counsel in an effort to agree on a new trial date. The sole purpose of a trial continuance would be to provide the parties sufficient time to conduct discovery regarding the request for punitive damages. Defendant's objections are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: QUINN EMANUEL URQUHART & SULLIVAN LLP VS. SUNLINK CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sun Link Corporation'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Request For Production (Set One), And Special Interrogatories (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 10, 2012, Line 21, Defendant Sun Link Corporation'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Request For Production (Set One), And Special Interrogatories (Set One)- This motion was heard by Judge Pro Tem David McDonald who, after a hearing on January 3, issued a report and recommendation, which will be emailed to the parties by 2pm today. The recommendation is to grant the motion as to interrogatories 63, 64, 65, 76, 77, 81, 84 and document request 34 limited to documents that concern references made on plaintiff's website in April 2008 with plaintiff required to serve further responses within 21 days and deny the motion in all other respects. The recommendation of Judge Pro Tem McDonald is adopted in full.
